description_long: This is a cylindrical stone water well with a robust, stacked-stone wall forming the main body. The well is topped with a pitched, wooden roof supported by two vertical wooden posts, providing shelter from above. The roof is constructed from wooden planks, some of which are fastened with visible metal bolts or rivets, giving it a rustic appearance. The interior of the well is open and dark, suggesting depth and the presence of air or water inside. There are no visible mechanisms for drawing water, such as a pulley or bucket, and the well lacks any decorative embellishments. The structure appears sturdy and utilitarian, suitable for placement in parks, courtyards, or other urban public spaces.
description: A cylindrical stone water well with a pitched wooden roof supported by two posts, featuring visible metal fasteners and an open, unadorned interior.
description_view_0: Front view showing the flat face of the wooden roof, two supporting posts, and the open stone well beneath.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the triangular profile of the roof and the circular stone wall.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with the wooden roof and open well clearly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view, again showing the pitched roof and stone structure from a different perspective.
attribute_well_cover_design: Pitched wooden roof made of planks with visible metal fasteners; no lid or grating over the well opening.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Cylindrical stone wall approximately waist-high, forming the main protective barrier around the well opening.
